explicit_text: I was born in Chicago, Illinois, on October 9th, 1926. Growing up on the South Side, I attended St. Rita High School there. Chicago was my home before I was drafted into the Army in January 1945, shortly after graduating high school in December 1944.

explicit_text: I went to St. Rita High School in Chicago, where we were known as the Mustangs. I graduated midyear in December 1944 so that I could be drafted immediately after. I also played the drums in the St. Rita band, which later influenced my initial placement in the Army Air Corps band.

explicit_text: After being drafted, I was sent to Fort Sheridan, Illinois, for initial processing about fifteen days after graduating high school. It was one of my first stops in the Army before heading to infantry training in Texas. Fort Sheridan was the gateway to my military service.

explicit_text: I trained at Camp Fannin in East Texas, near Tyler, for seventeen weeks of infantry training. This was my first major boot camp experience, where I prepared for the planned invasion of Japan. Camp Fannin was a key part of my early military life in 1945.

I was on the USS Bolivar heading to the Philippines when VJ Day occurred after the atomic bombings. The war shaped my service and my outlook, and I was proud to serve during such a pivotal time.

explicit_text: I was stationed in the Philippines after World War II ended, first arriving at Leyte and then Luzon, where I spent most of my time at Clark Air Base and in Manila. I developed a strong connection to the country, learning some of the local language and geography. The Philippines was where I recovered from tropical encephalitis and formed lasting memories and friendships.

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki (Event)
explicit_text: The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki occurred while I was en route to the Philippines. We only learned about them after arriving at Leyte, and the news was a huge relief because it meant the war was over and the planned invasion of Japan was canceled. It saved countless lives, including mine.
